description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly dark gray coloration and subtle variations in tone. The surface is rough and uneven, featuring natural striations and a few visible scratches, suggesting exposure to the elements. One side of the stone displays a small, white label with handwritten text, indicating it may have been cataloged or collected for study or landscaping. The stone is relatively flat and thin when viewed from the side, with jagged edges and a slightly angular profile. Its texture appears hard and gritty, typical of natural rock found in urban landscaping or as a barrier. The overall appearance suggests it could serve as a decorative or functional obstacle in parks, sidewalks, or garden paths.
description: A dark gray, rough-textured stone with jagged edges and a flat, thin profile, featuring a small white label on one side. Its natural striations and hard surface make it suitable as an urban barrier or landscaping element.
description_view_0: The stone appears broad and flat with visible striations and a rough, uneven surface.
description_view_1: The stone is seen edge-on, revealing its thin, slab-like profile and jagged vertical edges.
description_view_2: The back side shows a darker, more uniform surface with a white label containing handwritten text.
description_view_3: Another edge-on view, similar to view 1, highlighting the stone's thinness and irregular vertical contour.
attribute_location: urban park, landscaped sidewalk, or garden path
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Natural striations and subtle color mottling, with a small artificial label on one side
attribute_weathering: Rough and slightly scratched, indicating moderate exposure to weather but not heavily eroded
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other stones, but the label may create a slight visual contrast in manicured settings
